violations.text: Beef and Bean Chili, which was prepared in advance previous night, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Chili was observed at 55 degrees at the time of inspection. Chili was voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 05/03/2023

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods (Beef and Bean Chili). Per interview of PIC and Staff, Beef and Bean Chili was placed hot in the refrigerator in a container greater than four inches deep. Hand out for proper cooling methods left with PIC at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 05/03/2023

Working containers of cereals were not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. Food items were labeled at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/03/2023

The hand sink located directly next to the clean side of the dish machine table was observed to be lacking a splash guard preventing possible contamination of clean equipment/utensils in the dish area. Splash guard to be added to the hand sink/dish table end. New Violation. Correct By: 05/31/2023

At the time of inspection, the non-food contact surfaces of cookline equipment (Oven/Fryolator) were observed to have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 05/03/2023

The Ceiling in the kitchen area is in poor repair (observed to have a crack across the center of the kitchen. Crack in the ceiling is to be repaired. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/31/2023
